raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 1.0,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.7,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": false,
  "clarification_question": "",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening primarily requires sharpening the core thesis and contribution framing (e.g., what is first/novel and why it matters).",
    "R1.2": "Tightening the logical progression from problem setup to claims will make the argument more compelling and defensible.",
    "R1.3": "Claims like matching GPT-4-tier quality at lower cost and being the first demonstration must align precisely with the reported results and setup.",
    "R1.4": "To justify 'first' and 'reframes' assertions, the delta versus nearest prior routing and MoE/serving work needs to be articulated.",
    "R1.5": "Reordering or tightening the abstract’s narrative arc can foreground the key value proposition and evidence more effectively.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence-level clarity, concision, and tone adjustments will directly increase perceived strength without changing substance.",
    "RX.1": "Clear acknowledgment of assumptions, evaluation scope, and limits improves credibility when strengthening claims.",
    "RX.2": "Guarding against overreach in 'first' and 'deployment-ready' assertions avoids inflated or unsupportable statements.",
    "RX.3": "All changes must focus on strengthening the given artifact without expanding the task beyond its content.",
    "RX.4": "Avoiding language that could encourage unsafe or misleading deployment decisions mitigates downstream risk.",
    "RX.5": "Any strengthened statements should be traceable to specific parts of the provided text and stated results."
  }
}

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening primarily requires sharpening the core thesis and contribution framing (e.g., what is first/novel and why it matters).
rationale.R1.2: Tightening the logical progression from problem setup to claims will make the argument more compelling and defensible.
rationale.R1.3: Claims like matching GPT-4-tier quality at lower cost and being the first demonstration must align precisely with the reported results and setup.
rationale.R1.4: To justify 'first' and 'reframes' assertions, the delta versus nearest prior routing and MoE/serving work needs to be articulated.
rationale.R1.5: Reordering or tightening the abstract’s narrative arc can foreground the key value proposition and evidence more effectively.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence-level clarity, concision, and tone adjustments will directly increase perceived strength without changing substance.
rationale.RX.1: Clear acknowledgment of assumptions, evaluation scope, and limits improves credibility when strengthening claims.
rationale.RX.2: Guarding against overreach in 'first' and 'deployment-ready' assertions avoids inflated or unsupportable statements.
rationale.RX.3: All changes must focus on strengthening the given artifact without expanding the task beyond its content.
rationale.RX.4: Avoiding language that could encourage unsafe or misleading deployment decisions mitigates downstream risk.
rationale.RX.5: Any strengthened statements should be traceable to specific parts of the provided text and stated results.
